skill.name: shadcn-framer
skill.description: Provides ShadCN UI setup guidance and Framer Motion patterns for animated React components.
skill.summary: Build animated ShadCN UI components with Framer Motion patterns.
skill.author: Barnhardt-Enterprises-Inc

security_audit.summary: Static external-command hits mostly match Markdown code fences and TypeScript examples, so those are false positives. The setup section does contain real pnpm dlx commands that execute the latest ShadCN package, which is legitimate but carries supply-chain risk if run without review. The weak cryptography alerts are false positives from words such as description and CardDescription, with no cryptographic code found.

medium_findings.title: Unpinned External Package Execution
medium_findings.description: The setup instructions run pnpm dlx shadcn@latest init and add commands. This is normal for ShadCN setup, but it executes code from the package registry without pinning a reviewed version.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly present and would execute an external package if followed. The surrounding context is legitimate setup guidance, so this is supply-chain risk rather than evidence of malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fence Static False Positives
low_findings.description: The Ruby or shell backtick findings correspond to Markdown code block delimiters around examples. They do not indicate dynamic Ruby execution or hidden shell execution inside the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows these are fenced Markdown examples, not Ruby backtick execution. Only the setup block contains actual shell commands, which is handled separately.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts match ordinary prose and component identifiers, including description and CardDescription. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API usage is present in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain frontmatter text and React component names, not cryptographic operations. There is no semantic evidence of DES, MD5, SHA1, or similar weak algorithm use.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Setup Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes commands that install and run ShadCN tooling from the package registry. Users should review the package source and prefer pinned versions in controlled environments.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The external command is explicit and reproducible from the file. Its intent appears to be normal setup guidance, so the concern is operational risk.

content.value_statement: React teams need consistent UI components that still feel responsive and polished. This skill combines ShadCN patterns with Framer Motion examples for animated cards, lists, dialogs, transitions, and loading states.
actual_capabilities: Shows setup commands for initializing ShadCN UI and adding common components.
Provides React examples for ShadCN cards, buttons, and dialogs.
Demonstrates Framer Motion fade, hover, tap, and page transition patterns.
Includes animated list patterns using variants, staggered children, and AnimatePresence.
Shows a pulsing loading skeleton pattern using Framer Motion animation props.
limitations: Does not include a full project scaffold or package lock file.
Uses @latest in setup commands, so teams should pin versions before production use.
Does not include automated accessibility or reduced-motion checks.
Examples assume an existing React or Next.js project with TypeScript support.

use_cases.title: Prototype Animated Components
use_cases.description: Create quick ShadCN component prototypes with simple Framer Motion transitions.

use_cases.title: Standardize Motion Patterns
use_cases.description: Use shared examples for dialogs, cards, lists, and page transitions across an interface.

use_cases.title: Improve UI Feedback States
use_cases.description: Add hover, tap, and loading animations to make product screens feel more responsive.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Animated Card
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a ShadCN card with a Framer Motion fade-in animation for a user profile view.

prompt_templates.title: Animate a List View
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design an animated ShadCN list where items enter with staggered motion and exit smoothly.

prompt_templates.title: Add Motion to a Dialog
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to adapt a ShadCN dialog so its content opens and closes with Framer Motion while preserving the dialog API.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Motion Pattern Set
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to define reusable motion patterns for cards, dialogs, page transitions, skeletons, hover states, and list animations.

output_examples.input: Request an animated profile card using ShadCN UI and Framer Motion.
output_examples.output: A card pattern with header, description, content, footer action, and a short fade-in motion behavior.

output_examples.input: Request a dialog with entrance and exit animation.
output_examples.output: A dialog composition that keeps ShadCN structure while applying scale and opacity transitions to the dialog content.

output_examples.input: Request a loading state for a dashboard panel.
output_examples.output: A skeleton loading pattern that uses muted styling and repeated opacity changes for a subtle pulse effect.

best_practices: Pin package versions before running setup commands in production repositories.
Keep motion durations short and consistent across related components.
Check accessibility behavior, focus handling, and reduced-motion preferences after adding animations.
anti_patterns: Do not run @latest setup commands blindly in sensitive or locked-down repositories.
Do not animate dialog structure in a way that breaks focus management or escape behavior.
Do not apply large hover scale effects to dense layouts where content may shift.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build animated React components using ShadCN UI structure and Framer Motion behavior.

faq.question: Does it install ShadCN automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides setup commands that a user or coding agent may choose to run after review.

faq.question: Is it suitable for Next.js projects?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples use client components and import paths common in Next.js applications.

faq.question: Does it cover accessibility?
faq.answer: It relies on ShadCN dialog primitives but does not provide a complete accessibility testing workflow.

faq.question: Can it create reusable motion variants?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes container and item variant patterns that can be adapted for shared use.

faq.question: What security review is recommended?
faq.answer: Review and pin package versions before running pnpm dlx commands from the setup section.
